examples.text: فَأَمَّا مَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَازِينُهُ ۝ فَهُوَ فِي عِيشَةٍ رَّاضِيَةٍ ۝ وَأَمَّا مَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَازِينُهُ ۝ فَأُمُّهُ هَاوِيَةٌ ۝ وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا هِيَهْ ۝ نَارٌ حَامِيَةٌ

senses.gloss: to be burdensome, to be oppressive
to be sluggish, to be dull-witted
to be too sluggish, to be slow

examples.english: They are slow to do what we need.

senses.gloss: to load, to burden, to make heavy
to weigh; to determine the weight of an object
to be heavier than, to outweigh
to ask too much of, to overburden

examples.english: And the earth discharges its burdens

etymology.text:
From Proto-Semitic *ṯql (“to weigh; burden, weight”). Cognate with Hebrew שקל and Aramaic תקל.

Causative of ثَقُلَ (ṯaqula, “to be heavy”).

Compare Hebrew שֶׁקֶל (šéqel), Aramaic תִּקְלָא (tiqlā), Ugaritic 𐎘𐎖𐎍 (ṯql).

Inflected form.
